issues.description: CSV formula neutralization still omits leading LF (`\n`) from the dangerous-prefix set. A cell beginning with LF followed by a formula marker is only RFC-quoted, not apostrophe-prefixed, while LF is commonly treated as a CSV-injection prefix alongside tab/CR. P2-B is resolved, P3-C is resolved, and I agree P2-A can be deferred because the UI already renders `JSON.stringify(log.payload)` and the admin logs API returns the same raw payload to space admins.
issues.recommendation: Add `"\n"` to `FORMULA_INJECTION_PREFIXES` and add a regression test for a value like `"\n=SUM(1)"` expecting the apostrophe prefix inside the quoted field.

issues.description: Focused test execution was attempted but this read-only environment still fails before running tests with `Error: spawn EPERM` while loading `vitest.config.ts`.
issues.recommendation: Keep relying on the reported local full Vitest pass for gating, or rerun `npm test -- src/lib/csv.test.ts src/components/dashboard/csv-export.test.ts` in a normal writable/dev environment after the LF-prefix fix.
issues.deferRationale: Environment-only verification blocker; not a product defect in the reviewed code.
